title: hemerocallis fulva var. fulva
intro: Hemerocallis fulva var. fulva, also known as the Orange Daylily, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright orange, trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Hemerocallis fulva var. fulva include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in folk remedies for its antiseptic and antispasmodic properties.
